items.text: Just tried the new lavender honey latte at Grind House Café — best $4.50 I've spent all week! It's only on the menu through Friday, May 8. Grab one before your morning class. Seriously, the line moves fast and it's worth every single sip!

questions.stem: According to the post, until when is the new latte available?
questions.explanation: The post says the latte is on the menu only through Friday, May 8.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the new latte?

options.A: It is cheaper than other drinks on the menu.
options.B: It will not be sold after Friday this week.
options.C: It is sold during morning hours.
options.D: It comes with a free pastry.

questions.explanation: Because it is on the menu only through Friday, it can be inferred that the latte will not be sold after that day.

items.text:
Dear Ms. Alvarez,

The book you requested through interlibrary loan, "Coastal Ecology," arrived today. Please pick it up at the Circulation Desk in Hale Library by May 20. Remember to bring your student ID card. The loan period is twenty-one days.

Best,
Interlibrary Loan Office

questions.stem: According to the email, where should Ms. Alvarez pick up the book?

options.B: The reference shelf on the second floor
options.C: The Circulation Desk in Hale Library
options.D: The book drop near the main entrance

questions.explanation: The email tells Ms. Alvarez to pick up the book at the Circulation Desk in Hale Library.

questions.stem: According to the email, by what date must the book be picked up?
questions.explanation: The email states the book must be picked up by May 20.

items.text: Notice: Refunds for vending machine malfunctions are available at the Student Services window in Turner Hall, Room 110. Refunds are limited to $2 per transaction and must be claimed within seven days. The window is open weekdays from 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Report problems to Facilities at extension 4400.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the maximum refund per transaction?
questions.explanation: The notice states that refunds are limited to $2 per transaction.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who seeks a refund ten days after a malfunction?

options.A: They will not qualify for a refund.
options.B: They may still claim the full amount.
options.C: They must contact a different office.
options.D: They will receive a partial refund.

questions.explanation: Refunds must be claimed within seven days, so a request made after ten days would no longer qualify.

items.text: Notice: Heating and cooling systems in Baxter Hall will be serviced from March 9 to March 11. Indoor temperatures may rise noticeably during this period. Classes will continue as scheduled. Portable fans may be borrowed from the front desk for a $10 refundable deposit. Questions should be directed to Facilities.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what may students borrow during the service period?

options.A: A space heater for the classroom
options.D: A portable fan from the desk

questions.explanation: The notice says portable fans may be borrowed from the front desk for a refundable deposit.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about classes in Baxter Hall during the service period?

options.A: They will continue despite the warm rooms.
options.B: They will be moved to another building.
options.C: They will be canceled for three days.
options.D: They will begin at a later time.

questions.explanation: The notice states classes will continue as scheduled even though temperatures may rise, so classes go on despite the warmth.
